name: sim_autograd_deep_implicit_function_theorem_constraint -- autograd IFT via dg/dx = -(∂g/∂y)^-1 ∂g/∂x

pytorch.reason: autograd is load-bearing: both partial Jacobians ∂g/∂x, ∂g/∂y come from autograd; IFT claim rests on their invertibility

pyg.reason: graph message passing not required for this scalar/matrix-functional claim

z3.reason: claim is numerical-gradient structure, not SMT admissibility (deferred to companion proof sim)

cvc5.reason: same as z3: numerical gradient claim, not SMT

sympy.reason: symbolic form of entropy/Fisher/KL is standard; numerical gradient is the load-bearing object

clifford.reason: no Cl(n) rotor needed for scalar functional of rho

geomstats.reason: manifold metric not used; Fisher computed directly via autograd

rustworkx.reason: no graph structure in this probe

toponetx.reason: no cell complex in this probe

gudhi.reason: no persistent homology in this probe

scope_note: Constraint g(x,y) = x^2 + y^2 - 1 (and 2D extension); admissible where ∂g/∂y invertible.

singular_point_excluded.note: singular candidate excluded from IFT-admissible set
